All deliverables built, verified, and clean. Here is the plain-text summary. --- # WEB PRODUCER SURFACE — sealed-round scaffold complete Location: `/home/kamilner/tamarin-cleanroom/weblayer/producers/` Modeled on the `pretty/` scaffold. All deliverables present; stub crate compiles (`cargo test`: 1 pass + 2 ignored round-1 tests); oracle harness smoke-tested (start/probe/stop, port freed); round-1 loop validated byte-identically end-to-end. ## Target set (exact) — the ported PRODUCER files + their citations Determined by `grep` over `crates/tamarin-server/src/` + reading each header's citation list: | ported file | LOC | HS citation | header-blamed authors | in producer scope | |---|---|---|---|---| | `handlers/theory_html.rs` | 1012 | `Web/Theory.hs`, `Web/Hamlet.hs`, `Text/PrettyPrint/Html.hs` | meiersi, arcz, cascremers, felixlinker, beschmi, rsasse, jdreier, BTom-GH | R1+R2 | | `handlers/proof_tree.rs` | 1311 | `Web/Theory.hs` | jdreier, meiersi, racoucho1u, beschmi, charlie-j, rkunnema, felixlinker, rsasse, yavivanov, PhilipLukertWork, kevinmorio, arcz, BTom-GH, Nick Moore, ValentinYuri, addap, katrielalex, felixonmars | R3 | | `handlers/root.rs` | 216 | `Web/Hamlet.hs`, `Web/Handler.hs`, `Web/Types.hs` | meiersi, cascremers, arcz, jdreier, rsasse | R4 | | `handlers/path_parse.rs` | 241 | `Web/Types.hs` | meiersi, jdreier, arcz, **Kanakanajm**, rsasse, beschmi, felixlinker, addap | R5 | **Surprising / load-bearing finding on the pseudonymous authors:** the target files `theory_html.rs`/`proof_tree.rs`/`root.rs` do NOT carry Kanakanajm/YannColomb/Esslingen in their own headers — those three live on the DISPATCH shells `theory.rs`, `state.rs`, `lib.rs` (which the clean `web_clean` already covers but cannot delete). Only `path_parse.rs` carries a pseudonymous author (Kanakanajm, via `Web/Types.hs`) directly. Because `gen_license_headers.py` blames the shared `Web/{Types,Handler,Theory}.hs` sources cited across BOTH shells and producers, **no single producer deletion drops a pseudonymous author** — this matches the round-9 integration finding. The producers cluster is the *enabler*: landing R1–R5 makes all four producer files deletable, after which the already-clean-covered shells can also delete and the `Web/*.hs` citations (and Kanakanajm/YannColomb/Esslingen) vanish jointly. I documented this honestly in SPEC.md "Author topology" rather than overstating per-round yield. **Out of scope (confirmed):** `main/proof`/`main/method` (constraint system + applicable methods = solver), `main/cases` (sources = solver saturation), `interactive-graph-def`/`intdot`/`graph` (DOT/SVG = Unit B), the Rust-only `proof-step` route, `unload`, and the `source`/`message` plain-text theory echo (pretty cluster's byte surface). ## Decomposition (SPEC.md, ranked, each ALLOWLIST-gate-checkable) | # | sub-target | family (count) | isolation | retires | |---|---|---|---|---| | **R1** ⭐ | center section fragments + shared HTML skin (escape / `
`·` ` postprocess / `{html,title}` envelope) | `main/message`·`rules`·`tactic`·`help` (81 each) | HIGHEST — body 100% opaque; the skin R2/R3 reuse | part of `theory_html.rs` | | R2 | proof-script WEST pane (theory index assembly, lemma decl framing) | `overview` (473) | HIGH reuse / MODERATE (line grammar already in `web_clean::proofscript`) | rest of `theory_html.rs` | | R3 | proof-tree + method HTML (given the tree+method as input) | proof trees in proved overviews (396) | MODERATE — reuses R1 | `proof_tree.rs` | | R4 | welcome/index page + housekeeping | `/` + help static + robots/kill | VERY HIGH, small | `root.rs` | | R5 | theory-path grammar (URL⇄path) | link hrefs everywhere | HIGHEST (pure grammar) | `path_parse.rs` (carries Kanakanajm) | ## Round-1 recommendation: **R1 — center section fragments** Chosen over the mission's flagged proof-script pane because (a) each `main/
` is one route→one self-contained body with 100%-opaque content (sliceable straight from the capture), so the producer's job is purely the frame/postprocess/envelope; (b) that skin is the leaf R2/R3 reuse — de-risking everything downstream, the pretty-R1 philosophy; (c) the west pane's line grammar is *already* clean (`web_clean::proofscript`), it reuses R1's skin and needs R5's links, so it composes better as R2. Documented in SPEC.md. ## Big finding — the acceptance gate is SEMANTIC, not byte-identity `scripts/web_parity.sh`/`web_normalize.py` canonicalize away `hl_*` spans, `
`, ` `, attribute/JSON-key order, DOT serialization and volatile env fields, then diff structure + visible text + hrefs + form actions + headings + JSON values. This materially differs from the pretty cluster's byte gate and reshapes what the producer must nail (structure/links/headings/titles, not the exact skin). Called out prominently in SPEC.md and BEHAVIOR.md, and folded into the acceptance ladder (fixtures → self-contained capture-corpus replay → `web_parity.sh` full crawl). ## Oracle status — VERIFIED, and I fixed a real bug - `oracle/hs_server.sh` (rewritten wrapper): the stock weblayer `hs_server.sh` is broken — it passes `--port ` with a SPACE, which the prover's CmdArgs parser reads as the positional WORKDIR (`directory '' does not exist`), so the server dies silently after its fixed `sleep 4`. My wrapper uses `--port=` (equals, like `web_parity.sh`), POLLS for readiness, keeps the OOM guards (`oom_score_adj`+24 GB `ulimit`), and frees the port on stop (pid + process-group + `fuser` fallback + workdir cleanup). Smoke-tested: `smoke Tutorial 3120` boots in ~1 s, serves overview+message, frees the port. Ports 3100-3199. - `oracle/extract_fragments.py` — captures-only slicer (`list`/`families`/`extract [--only …]`), single corpus pass, labels by theory-name+hash (10 name collisions handled). - `oracle/captured_responses/` — symlink to `.web_hs_cache` (the PROTOCOL-sanctioned channel; weblayer precedent). All 81 manifests map cleanly to corpus sources by `sha256`. - **End-to-end validation:** live oracle on `oracle/examples/issue515.spthy` reproduces the captured `main/message` body **byte-identically** — targets are trustworthy. ## Round-1 materials `round1/families.tsv` (11 curated labels spanning signature/xor/multiset/restriction variety), `round1/fetch_targets.sh` (single-pass), `round1/targets/` = **44 fragment byte targets** (11 labels × message/rules/tactic/help, `.html`+`.title`+`.meta.tsv`). ## Barrier hygiene `interface/fragment_inputs.rs` + `required_api.md` convey BEHAVIOR only — tripwire-checked clean: no `.hs` citations, no `Web/`, no upstream render-function names (`theoryIndex`/`lemmaIndex`/`ppSection`/`markStatus`/`subProofSnippet`/`prettyProof`/`_snippet`), no template splice-anchor names (`proof_state`/`main_view`). Defensively renamed my own `TheoryIndex`→`ProofScriptPane`, `IndexItem`→`NavItem` to avoid any echo. Types are behavior-named; byte targets are captured OUTPUT only. No implementer launched. No stray servers/ports; temp files cleaned. Key paths: `/home/kamilner/tamarin-cleanroom/weblayer/producers/{SPEC.md,README.md}`, `.../oracle/{hs_server.sh,extract_fragments.py}`, `.../interface/{fragment_inputs.rs,required_api.md}`, `.../workspace/producers-clean/`, `.../round1/`.

ROUND 1 (R1: center section fragments + shared HTML skin) — COMPLETE, all gates green. IMPLEMENTED (all inside /home/kamilner/tamarin-cleanroom/weblayer/producers/workspace/): - producers-clean/src/html.rs — the shared skin: escape_text (&→& <→< >→> "→" '→'; backslash passes through — forced live via an own-authored theory served from the metachar filename esc&"<>'probe.spthy); postprocess_lines (every logical line, first/last/empty alike, → text +
\n, leading space runs →   runs, interior spaces untouched); the three envelopes {html,title} (compact, html key first), {redirect}, {alert}, with JSON escaping pinned to the full-corpus inventory (\" \\ \n \t only; other C0 unobserved, standard forms documented as arbitrary choice). - producers-clean/src/section.rs — render_pane (message/rules/tactic block skeleton:

H

line +

glued to first body line,

to last; blocks adjacent with no blank line) and render_help_pane (env line + invariant static block, single-line, no postprocess, no trailing newline; title "Theory: NAME"). src/help_static.html = the byte-invariant-across-81 static block, verbatim captured output. - producers-clean/src/model.rs — added EmptyRender {Keep, BlankLine, Omit} (all three modes observed) and HelpPane; interface mapping documented. - tools/extract_r1_raw.py + r1_raw/ (324 raw envelope bodies) + r1_live/ (8 live-probed bodies). KEY BEHAVIORAL FINDINGS (BEHAVIOR.md §§2–9, QUERIES.log [S07]–[S13],[L03]–[L07]): 1. The rules-pane title is the CONSTANT "Multiset rewriting rules and restrictions" across all 81 captures — the seeded BEHAVIOR.md claim that " and restrictions" is conditional is REFUTED and corrected. 2. The corpus-wide leading
in main/rules is the EMPTY MACROS SLOT: live probe on MacroGlobalVarNSPK3 (macros never occur in the corpus) shows the pane starting directly with

Macros

; absent macros leave exactly one blank line. Restrictions instead vanish without residue and join the MSR block with no blank. 3. Empty body ⇒ empty

is the skin's behavior (tactic); the injective-facts "None" is therefore INPUT, not skin. 4. {alert} is absent from the corpus; forced live via del/path/help ({"alert":"Can't delete the given theory path!"}). ACCEPTANCE: cargo test = 14 tests green, zero warnings, std-only. corpus_sweep_all_manifests: 324/324 fragments (81 manifests × 4 families) byte-identical after slice-and-re-render, ENVELOPE INCLUDED; round1_materialized_targets: 44/44; live_probe_replays: 8/8 on two never-captured theories (incl. the only macros-present rules pane observed anywhere). Mutation check confirms the sweep fails on a doctored break marker (gate not vacuous). UNOBSERVABLES (recorded, choices documented): empty message-pane sections (Keep assumed by analogy with tactic); tabs/C0 controls beyond \n in fragments; heading and NAME/TIME escaping (metachar-free vocabularies). DISCIPLINE: every oracle interaction logged in workspace/QUERIES.log ([S01]–[S13],[L01]–[L07]); BEHAVIOR.md carries the full derivable spec with per-claim provenance; no file under tamarin-rs/crates or any Haskell source read; oracle OOM guards untouched; all servers stopped (ports 3100–3199 scanned clean, no pidfiles); no git commits; nothing outside weblayer/ modified (probe .spthy in the session scratchpad only). Ready for R2 (proof-script west pane); integrator's next step is scripts/web_parity.sh with RS_PATH at the workspace build.

Both audits complete; the producers round-1 section is appended to `/home/kamilner/tamarin-cleanroom/weblayer/AUDIT.md`. AUDIT 1 — barrier hygiene of the open-authored scaffold: - The precedent vector is CLEAN: `interface/required_api.md` and `interface/fragment_inputs.rs` leak no upstream/port identifiers (indicative names only; explicit "not a transcription"). `round1/` + `oracle/` operate on captured OUTPUT only. A scan for ~35 upstream Haskell *function* identifiers across the whole scaffold returns zero — no expression-bearing name reaches sealed-readable text. - FINDING PH-1 (scaffold rewrite required): `SPEC.md` (and `README.md`) cite upstream source-file paths — `src/Web/{Theory,Hamlet,Handler,Types}.hs` and `Text/PrettyPrint/Html.hs` — in the "HS citation it advances" column and Author-topology text. This diverges from the sibling norm (`pretty`/`graphdot`/`wellformedness` SPECs cite zero upstream files, doing the same citation-yield/author-topology via the *ported* `.rs` seams + author lists). Rewrite: recast those onto `handlers/theory_html.rs`/`proof_tree.rs`/`root.rs`/`path_parse.rs` + the dispatch shells (as `pretty/SPEC.md` does) and delete every `src/Web/*.hs` string. It is file-level bookkeeping only — no function name, decomposition, anchor, or expression — so it does not repeat the splice-anchor precedent and did not contaminate the sealed work. - Within-norm (not findings): port-seam file/fn names (`render_proof_tree_html`, `proof_state`, `web_clean::proofscript`) + pseudonym topology match `pretty/SPEC.md`; `withHeader` (SPEC line 85) is a phantom (absent from the entire upstream tree), harmless. AUDIT 2 — similarity of the sealed crate (`workspace/producers-clean/`): 0 violations. R1 is the only implemented surface; R2–R5 are `unimplemented!()` stubs. All resemblance to `Theory.hs`/`Text/PrettyPrint/Html.hs`/`Handler.hs` is observable served output (tag skeleton, heading/title vocabulary, `{html,title}` envelope, verbatim help block, help env line) — merger/compatibility content pinned by the 324/324 corpus-sweep byte gate. The skin's *expression* diverges materially: `split('\n')`+`trim_start_matches(' ')`+byte-count vs upstream `lines`/`unlines`+`span isSpace`+`concatMap` (spaces-only vs tab-inclusive); a unified 3-variant `EmptyRender` enum vs upstream's `caseEmptyDoc`/`if null … text empty`/plain-`ppSection` mix; a hand-rolled envelope+`json_escape_into` vs Aeson `object`. Zero upstream-internal identifier overlap; no comment lineage (`blaze`/"Build the Html document"/"converts the line-breaks" absent); source-only branches (`pp` empty-string bug guard, `if null injFacts then "None"`) not reproduced; rendered-byte artifacts (stray ``) reproduced byte-exactly. Dispositive provenance: the unconditional rules-title constant "Multiset rewriting rules and restrictions" (Theory.hs:1589, emitted even with zero restrictions) is documented in BEHAVIOR.md §6 as a *seeded hypothesis that observation REFUTED* — proof of black-box derivation. QUERIES.log [S01]–[S13]/[L01]–[L07] (corpus sweeps + live oracle on an own-authored EscProbe theory) records no source read. Non-blocking: shipped-comment provenance narration (`[S07]`/`[L03]`/`BEHAVIOR.md §` in `src/*.rs`) — recurring Round-6/7 hygiene note, opposite of copied expression, no verdict effect. Net: similarity 0; one non-contaminating scaffold-hygiene finding (PH-1) with a rewrite instruction. The pristine interface contract + the affirmatively independent crate show the barrier held where it counts. VERDICT: pass
